content.content:
Task #1: Fix a11y: always-in-DOM error elements in login, todos, and edit_todo templates
Status: in_progress
Description: Heydon's a11y review found that error elements in login.html, todos.html, and edit_todo.html are conditionally rendered (only present when there's an error). They should ALWAYS be in the DOM (empty when no error) with role="alert" so aria-describedby resolves and screen readers can announce errors when they appear. The register.html template already follows this pattern correctly — use it as the reference. Specifically:

- login.html: The generic error message div (line 8-11) should always be present, empty when no error
- todos.html: The add-todo error div (line 30-33) should always be present, empty when no error  
- edit_todo.html: Both the general error div (line 14-17) and the title-error element (line 32-34) should always be present with aria-describedby on the input always referencing them, and aria-invalid only when there IS an error

Follow TDD: write a failing test first, then fix, then refactor. Run cargo fmt, clippy, and tests before committing. Commit and push immediately when green.

1→use sqlx::PgPool;
     2→
     3→use crate::domain::{TodoItem, TodoItemId, TodoTitle, TodoTitleError, UserId};
     4→use crate::infrastructure::todo_repository;
     5→
     6→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
     7→pub enum AddTodoError {
     8→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
     9→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
    10→    #[error(transparent)]
    11→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
    12→}
    13→
    14→#[tracing::instrument(
    15→    name = "add_todo",
    16→    skip(pool, title),
    17→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    18→)]
    19→pub async fn add_todo(
    20→    pool: &PgPool,
    21→    user_id: UserId,
    22→    title: String,
    23→) -> Result<TodoItem, AddTodoError> {
    24→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(title)?;

1→use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
     2→use sqlx::PgPool;
     3→use uuid::Uuid;
     4→
     5→use crate::domain::{TodoItem, TodoItemId, TodoTitle, UserId};
     6→
     7→pub struct TodoRecord {
     8→    pub id: Uuid,
     9→    pub user_id: Uuid,
    10→    pub title: String,
    11→    pub completed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    12→    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    13→}
    14→
    15→impl TodoRecord {
    16→    pub fn to_domain(self) -> TodoItem {
    17→        let id = TodoItemId::from_uuid(self.id);
    18→        let user_id = UserId::from_uuid(self.user_id);
    19→        // Title was validated on insert, so we trust it here
    20→        let title = TodoTitle::parse(self.title).expect("stored title should be valid");
    21→
    22→        match self.completed_at {
    23→            Some(completed_at) => TodoItem::Completed {
    24→                id,
    25→                user_id,
    26→                title,
    27→                created_at: self.created_at,
    28→                completed_at,
    29→            },
    30→            None => TodoItem::Pending {
    31→                id,
    32→                user_id,
    33→                title,
    34→                created_at: self.created_at,
    35→            },
    36→        }
    37→    }
    38→}
    39→
    40→pub async fn insert_todo(pool: &PgPool, item: &TodoItem) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    41→    let (completed_at, created_at) = match item {
    42→        TodoItem::Pending { created_at, .. } => (None, *created_at),
    43→        TodoItem::Completed {
    44→            created_at,
    45→            completed_at,
    46→            ..
    47→        } => (Some(*completed_at), *created_at),
    48→    };
    49→
    50→    sqlx::query!(
    51→        r#"INSERT INTO todos (id, user_id, title, completed_at, created_at)
    52→           VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4 :: timestamptz, $5 :: timestamptz)"#,
    53→        item.id().as_uuid(),
    54→        item.user_id().as_uuid(),
    55→        item.title().as_str(),
    56→        completed_at as Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    57→        created_at as DateTime<Utc>,
    58→    )
    59→    .execute(pool)
    60→    .await?;
    61→    Ok(())
    62→}
    63→
    64→pub async fn find_todos_by_user(
    65→    pool: &PgPool,
    66→    user_id: &UserId,
    67→) -> Result<Vec<TodoItem>, sqlx::Error> {
    68→    let records = sqlx::query_as!(
    69→        TodoRecord,
    70→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, title,
    71→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    72→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    73→           FROM todos WHERE user_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at ASC"#,
    74→        user_id.as_uuid(),
    75→    )
    76→    .fetch_all(pool)
    77→    .await?;
    78→
    79→    Ok(records.into_iter().map(|r| r.to_domain()).collect())
    80→}
    81→
    82→pub async fn find_todo_by_id(
    83→    pool: &PgPool,
    84→    todo_id: &TodoItemId,
    85→) -> Result<Option<TodoItem>, sqlx::Error> {
    86→    let record = sqlx::query_as!(
    87→        TodoRecord,
    88→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, title,
    89→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    90→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    91→           FROM todos WHERE id = $1"#,
    92→        todo_id.as_uuid(),
    93→    )
    94→    .fetch_optional(pool)
    95→    .await?;
    96→
    97→    Ok(record.map(|r| r.to_domain()))
    98→}
    99→
   100→pub async fn delete_todo(pool: &PgPool, todo_id: &TodoItemId) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   101→    sqlx::query!(r#"DELETE FROM todos WHERE id = $1"#, todo_id.as_uuid(),)
   102→        .execute(pool)
   103→        .await?;
   104→    Ok(())
   105→}
   106→
   107→pub async fn update_todo_completion(
   108→    pool: &PgPool,
   109→    todo_id: &TodoItemId,
   110→    completed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
   111→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   112→    sqlx::query!(
   113→        r#"UPDATE todos SET completed_at = $1 :: timestamptz WHERE id = $2"#,
   114→        completed_at as Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
   115→        todo_id.as_uuid(),
   116→    )
   117→    .execute(pool)
   118→    .await?;
   119→    Ok(())
   120→}
   121→
   122→pub async fn update_todo_title(
   123→    pool: &PgPool,
   124→    todo_id: &TodoItemId,
   125→    title: &str,
   126→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   127→    sqlx::query!(
   128→        r#"UPDATE todos SET title = $1 WHERE id = $2"#,
   129→        title,
   130→        todo_id.as_uuid(),
   131→    )
   132→    .execute(pool)

I now have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me summarize what I've learned and outline the detailed implementation plan for Task #3.

Here's the current state and my plan:

**Current architecture:**
- Plain HTML forms with POST + redirect pattern (303 See Other)
- Templates: `base.html` (layout), `todos.html` (list page), `edit_todo.html` (separate edit page)
- Handlers return full pages via Askama templates
- CSS already has HTMX indicator styles prepared
- Base template already has `aria-live="polite"` region with id `live-region`
- No HTMX script included yet

**HTMX Enhancement Plan (Task #3):**

1. **Include HTMX**: Download `htmx.min.js` to `static/js/`, add script tag to `base.html`
2. **Extract fragment templates**: Create partial Askama templates for individual components
3. **HX-Request detection**: Add header check to handlers so they return fragments for HTMX requests, full pages for regular requests
4. **Add todo**: `hx-post="/todos"`, target `#todo-list`, swap `beforeend`, clear input after success
5. **Toggle**: `hx-post` on toggle form, `hx-target="closest li"`, `hx-swap="outerHTML"` to replace item in place
6. **Delete**: `hx-post` on delete confirm form, `hx-target="closest li"`, `hx-swap="delete"` to remove
7. **Edit**: Edit link gets `hx-get` to fetch inline edit form, save gets `hx-post` to replace back

I'm ready to drive. Waiting for the team lead to confirm Tasks #1 and #2 are complete (or to tell me to go ahead). Per commit discipline, only one person drives at a time.
